# Tasks — Podcast Summary Skill
*Generated by create_tasks on 2026-03-21*

## Objective
Build a nightly podcast summarization pipeline that monitors RSS feeds, acquires transcripts via scraping or local Whisper, generates adaptive LLM summaries, delivers email digests, ingests health newsletters from Gmail, and maintains a persistent health knowledge store.

## Scope
- **In Scope**: RSS feed monitoring, OPML import, Whisper bridge (host service), transcript pipeline, email digest, Gmail IMAP newsletter ingestion, health knowledge store, 6AM notification, on-demand episode summaries, semantic episode search, show classification learning
- **Out of Scope**: Overcast/Spotify automation, health DB query interface, Telegram inline summaries, multi-user support, non-English podcasts, additional newsletters beyond Attia + Rhonda Patrick

## Requirements Traced

| ID | Description | Tasks |
|----|-------------|-------|
| REQ-001 | OPML import for initial feed list | 1.2 |
| REQ-002 | Show state management (active/one-off/inactive) | 1.3, 1.2 |
| REQ-003 | Add/remove shows via Telegram | 4.4 |
| REQ-004 | 11PM system crontab nightly trigger | 1.6 |
| REQ-005 | Overnight batch: RSS poll → transcript → summarize | 2.4 |
| REQ-006 | Email digest delivery | 2.3 |
| REQ-007 | 6AM Telegram/iMessage notification (podcasts + newsletters bundled) | 2.5 |
| REQ-008 | Adaptive summary style per show type | 2.4 |
| REQ-009 | Transparent sourcing label on every summary | 2.3 |
| REQ-010 | Local Whisper via host bridge (whisper.cpp + Metal) | 1.1 |
| REQ-011 | Per-show Whisper model (large-v3 for health, small.en default) | 2.2 |
| REQ-012 | Transcript acquisition pipeline (scrape → Whisper → show notes) | 2.1, 2.2 |
| REQ-013 | Per-show transcript strategy cached in feeds.json | 2.1 |
| REQ-014 | On-demand specific episode summary (name/number or URL) | 4.1 |
| REQ-015 | Vague/semantic episode search | 4.2 |
| REQ-016 | Re-summarize at extended depth on request | 4.3 |
| REQ-017 | Health knowledge store (health_knowledge.json) | 3.1 |
| REQ-018 | Health tagging: always/sometimes/never + per-episode override | 3.2 |
| REQ-019 | Gmail IMAP ingestion of health-newsletters label | 3.3 |
| REQ-020 | Newsletter full content fetch (follow Attia teaser links) | 3.4 |
| REQ-021 | Move processed newsletters to Gmail Trash | 3.3 |
| REQ-022 | Health newsletters archived to health_knowledge.json | 3.4 |
| REQ-023 | No emails until Phase 2 (Whisper) complete | 1.5 |
| REQ-024 | Show classification auto-learned + correctable via Telegram | 4.5 |
| REQ-025 | Whisper bridge always-on (LaunchAgent) | 1.1 |
| REQ-026 | OpenClaw skill enabled in config | 2.5 |

---

## Architecture Context

### Where This Fits
- New skill at `workspace/skills/podcast-summary/` — follows the same directory structure as `restaurant-scout`, `productwatcher`, and `daily-report`
- Host-side scripts run directly on the Mac (not in container) — same pattern as `audio_bridge_service.py` and `learning-log-bridge.sh`
- Vault JSON files live in `workspace/skills/podcast-summary/podcast_vault/` — mounted volume means host and container see the same files
- OpenClaw agent handles on-demand requests and 6AM notifications; Mac host handles all batch processing

### Technical Approach
- **Vault pattern** from `productwatcher/scripts/watcher_engine.py` — three JSON files, atomic writes via `os.replace(tmp)`
- **Host bridge pattern** from `workspace/audio/audio_bridge_service.py` — Python HTTP service on `0.0.0.0:18797`, reached via `host.docker.internal:18797` from container
- **Email delivery** reuses `workspace/skills/send-email/scripts/send_email.py` directly — no reimplementation
- **Logging** cloned from `restaurant-scout/scripts/scout-log.sh` — structured JSON to `/tmp/openclaw/podcast-summary.log`
- Summarization calls OpenAI API directly from engine.py (uses existing `OPENAI_API_KEY` from `.env`) — avoids agent turn timeout risk for batch jobs

### Key Decisions
- Batch engine runs on Mac host (system crontab) not inside OpenClaw — eliminates turn timeout risk for 7-hour overnight windows
- No emails until Phase 2 (Whisper) is live — quality bar set at transcript-level summaries from day one
- Health store is JSON not SQLite — ~500 entries/year is well within JSON+LLM context window approach for future queries
- Whisper model routing: `large-v3` for Attia/Huberman/Rhonda Patrick, `small.en` for all others — configurable per-show in `feeds.json`
- Whisper bridge stays always-on (LaunchAgent) — needed for on-demand requests from container at any time

---

## Tasks

---

### Phase 1: Core Infrastructure (no emails — setup and validation only)

---

#### [1.1] Whisper Bridge Host Service

- [ ] **1.1.1** Build whisper.cpp from source on Mac host with Metal support
  - **Produces**: `whisper-server` binary at `whisper.cpp/build/bin/whisper-server`
  - **Consumed by**: 1.1.2 (LaunchAgent), 2.2 (whisper_client.py)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new)
  - [ ] `cmake --build` completes without error on macOS; Metal detected in build output
  - [ ] `whisper-server` binary exists and starts with `./whisper-server --help`
  - [ ] Both `ggml-small.en.bin` and `ggml-large-v3.bin` model files downloaded to `whisper.cpp/models/`

- [ ] **1.1.2** Create macOS LaunchAgent plist for always-on whisper-server
  - **Produces**: `scripts/launchagents/com.ironclaw.whisper-bridge.plist` (template with placeholders)
  - **Consumed by**: `launchctl load` during setup; engine.py health check
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new)
  - [ ] Plist starts `whisper-server` on `0.0.0.0:18797` with `--convert` flag and `small.en` as default model; `KeepAlive=true`
  - [ ] `OPERATIONS.md` documents `launchctl load/unload` commands and model path substitution
  - [ ] `curl http://localhost:18797/` returns a response (HTML test UI) confirming server is running

- [ ] **1.1.3** Create whisper bridge setup script
  - **Produces**: `scripts/setup-whisper-bridge.sh` — automates build + model download + LaunchAgent install
  - **Consumed by**: operator running setup per `OPERATIONS.md`
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new)
  - [ ] Script checks for `cmake`, `git`, `ffmpeg` prerequisites and prints clear error if missing
  - [ ] Script accepts `--model-dir` and `--port` arguments with documented defaults
  - [ ] Idempotent: safe to re-run if already installed (checks before overwriting)

---

#### [1.2] OPML Importer + Feed Registry Initialization

- [ ] **1.2.1** Create `importer.py` — OPML → feeds.json bulk loader
  - **Produces**: `scripts/importer.py`; populates `podcast_vault/feeds.json` from OPML
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py (reads feeds.json on every run)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (initial seed)
  - [ ] Parses OPML `<outline type="rss">` entries, extracts `text` (show name) and `xmlUrl` (RSS URL)
  - [ ] Creates feeds.json with all feeds set to `state: "active"`, `summary_style: null` (auto-classify later), `health_tier: null`, `whisper_model: null`, `transcript_strategy: []`
  - [ ] Merge-safe: re-running with updated OPML adds new feeds without overwriting existing entries' state or learned metadata

- [ ] **1.2.2** Pre-seed feeds.json from the Overcast OPML export
  - **Produces**: `podcast_vault/feeds.json` with initial feed list
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py, rss_poller.py
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] All feeds from OPML are present with correct RSS URLs
  - [ ] Known health shows (`peter-attia-drive`, `huberman-lab`, `found-my-fitness`) have `health_tier: "always"` and `whisper_model: "large-v3"` pre-set
  - [ ] Known commentary/hunting shows have appropriate `health_tier: "never"` pre-set

- [ ] **1.2.3** Document `--dry-run` flag for importer
  - **Produces**: `--dry-run` flag in importer.py that prints what would be written without touching feeds.json
  - **Consumed by**: operator validation before first real import
  - [ ] `python3 importer.py feeds.opml --dry-run` prints all feeds that would be added/skipped without writing
  - [ ] Output clearly distinguishes new feeds vs already-existing entries

---

#### [1.3] Vault Structure + Data Schemas

- [ ] **1.3.1** Create `podcast_vault/` directory structure and initialize empty vault files
  - **Produces**: `podcast_vault/feeds.json` (from 1.2), `podcast_vault/episodes.json`, `podcast_vault/health_knowledge.json`, `podcast_vault/processing_status.json`
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py, rss_poller.py, health_store.py, digest_emailer.py
  - [ ] All four vault files exist with valid empty JSON skeletons (`{"version": 1, "episodes": []}` etc.)
  - [ ] `podcast_vault/` directory is gitignored (contains runtime state, not source)
  - [ ] A `podcast_vault/README.md` documents each file's schema and retention policy

- [ ] **1.3.2** Create `vault.py` — shared vault load/save utilities with atomic writes
  - **Produces**: `scripts/vault.py` with `load_vault(file)` and `save_vault(file, data)` functions
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py, health_store.py, rss_poller.py, gmail_fetcher.py
  - [ ] `save_vault()` writes to `.tmp` file then `os.replace()` — never corrupts on crash
  - [ ] `load_vault()` returns empty schema on missing file (first-run safe)
  - [ ] Both functions handle `json.JSONDecodeError` gracefully with a logged warning and empty fallback

---

#### [1.4] RSS Poller

- [ ] **1.4.1** Create `rss_poller.py` — RSS/Atom feed parser using Python stdlib only
  - **Produces**: `scripts/rss_poller.py`; returns list of new episode dicts for a given feed
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py `check_new_episodes()`
  - [ ] Parses RSS 2.0 and Atom feeds using `xml.etree.ElementTree`; handles both formats
  - [ ] Extracts per-episode: `title`, `pub_date` (ISO 8601 via `email.utils.parsedate_to_datetime`), `audio_url` (from `<enclosure>`), `duration_seconds`, `description`, `full_notes` (from `content:encoded`), `transcript_tag_url` (from `podcast:transcript` namespace), `guid`
  - [ ] Returns only episodes with `pub_date` newer than `feed.last_episode_guid` — no reprocessing already-seen episodes

- [ ] **1.4.2** Add User-Agent header and redirect handling to rss_poller
  - **Produces**: Updated `rss_poller.py` with robust HTTP fetching
  - **Consumed by**: all feed fetches
  - [ ] Sends `User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0` header to avoid CDN 403s
  - [ ] `urllib.request` follows redirects automatically; logs final URL on redirect
  - [ ] Private RSS feed URLs (Attia Supercast, FoundMyFitness) fetch correctly without extra auth

---

#### [1.5] Skill Scaffold (SKILL.md + OPERATIONS.md)

- [ ] **1.5.1** Create `SKILL.md` — OpenClaw skill definition for on-demand pipeline
  - **Produces**: `workspace/skills/podcast-summary/SKILL.md`
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent during on-demand turns
  - [ ] Frontmatter includes `name`, `description`, `metadata.openclaw.emoji`, `metadata.openclaw.requires.bins: ["bash", "python3", "curl"]`
  - [ ] On-demand pipeline steps defined: classify intent → find episode → acquire transcript → summarize → email result
  - [ ] Explicit instruction: batch processing (nightly digest) is handled by host-side engine.py, not this skill

- [ ] **1.5.2** Create `OPERATIONS.md` — setup runbook
  - **Produces**: `workspace/skills/podcast-summary/OPERATIONS.md`
  - **Consumed by**: operator during initial setup
  - [ ] Documents all setup steps in order: whisper.cpp build, model downloads, LaunchAgent install, crontab entry, `.env` keys, OPML import
  - [ ] Documents all new `.env` keys with descriptions and example values (no real secrets)
  - [ ] Documents how to add a new podcast feed via Telegram command

- [ ] **1.5.3** Create `podcast-log.sh` — structured logging script
  - **Produces**: `scripts/podcast-log.sh` (clone of `restaurant-scout/scripts/scout-log.sh`)
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py, on_demand.py via exec
  - [ ] Writes structured JSON log lines to `/tmp/openclaw/podcast-summary.log`
  - [ ] Supports events: `engine_start`, `rss_poll`, `transcript_fetch`, `whisper_transcribe`, `summarize`, `digest_send`, `newsletter_fetch`, `health_store`, `engine_done`, `error`
  - [ ] Uses `python3` for all JSON building (no `jq`)

---

#### [1.6] Engine Coordinator (dry-run mode only in Phase 1)

- [ ] **1.6.1** Create `engine.py` skeleton with env loading, vault loading, and dry-run mode
  - **Produces**: `scripts/engine.py` — main coordinator
  - **Consumed by**: system crontab at 11PM
  - [ ] Reads all credentials from `agents/{agent_name}/.env` via `python-dotenv` or manual parsing (no external deps needed — parse `KEY=VALUE` lines with stdlib)
  - [ ] Loads `feeds.json` and `episodes.json` from vault via `vault.py`
  - [ ] `--dry-run` flag: calls rss_poller, prints new episodes found, writes nothing, sends nothing

- [ ] **1.6.2** Implement `check_new_episodes()` in engine.py
  - **Produces**: list of new episode dicts for all active feeds
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py main loop → transcript acquisition (Phase 2)
  - [ ] Iterates all feeds with `state: "active"`; calls `rss_poller.poll(feed)` for each
  - [ ] Skips feeds with errors (logs warning, continues); does not abort full run on single feed failure
  - [ ] Updates `feed.last_checked` timestamp in feeds.json after each successful poll

---

#### [1.7] System Crontab + Host Setup

- [ ] **1.7.1** Document and install Mac system crontab entries
  - **Produces**: Two crontab entries documented in `OPERATIONS.md` + install instructions
  - **Consumed by**: Mac host scheduler
  - [ ] `0 23 * * *` entry runs `engine.py` nightly (11PM)
  - [ ] Crontab entry includes full absolute path to python3 and engine.py; pipes stdout/stderr to dated log file in `agents/{name}/logs/podcast-engine-YYYYMMDD.log`
  - [ ] Instructions cover how to verify crontab is installed (`crontab -l`) and how to test with `--dry-run`

---

### Phase 2: Transcript Pipeline + Email Digest (emails begin)

---

#### [2.1] Transcript Fetcher — Scraping Strategies

- [ ] **2.1.1** Create `transcript_fetcher.py` with per-show strategy dispatch
  - **Produces**: `scripts/transcript_fetcher.py`; returns `(transcript_text, source_quality)` tuple
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py `acquire_content(episode)`
  - [ ] Dispatches to correct strategy based on `feed.transcript_strategy` list in feeds.json; tries strategies in order until one succeeds
  - [ ] On strategy success: caches result in `feed.transcript_strategy` with `last_tested` date so future runs skip failed strategies
  - [ ] On all strategies fail: returns `(full_notes_or_description, "show_notes")` as final fallback — never returns empty

- [ ] **2.1.2** Implement Tim Ferriss transcript fetcher (`fetch_tim_blog`)
  - **Produces**: strategy function in transcript_fetcher.py
  - **Consumed by**: 2.1.1 strategy dispatch for `tim-ferriss-show`
  - [ ] Constructs transcript URL from episode slug: `https://tim.blog/YYYY/MM/DD/{slug}-transcript/`
  - [ ] Falls back to fetching `tim.blog/feed/` and scanning for "transcript" posts matching episode title if direct URL fails
  - [ ] Returns full transcript text stripped of HTML tags

- [ ] **2.1.3** Implement Huberman transcript fetcher (`fetch_podscript_ai`)
  - **Produces**: strategy function in transcript_fetcher.py
  - **Consumed by**: 2.1.1 strategy dispatch for `huberman-lab`
  - [ ] Fetches `https://podscript.ai/podcasts/huberman-lab-podcast/{slug}` and extracts transcript text
  - [ ] Detects 403/bot-protection response and logs failure to `feed.transcript_strategy` cache
  - [ ] Returns plain text transcript stripped of navigation/UI HTML

- [ ] **2.1.4** Implement HappyScribe transcript fetcher (`fetch_happyscribe`) for Attia
  - **Produces**: strategy function in transcript_fetcher.py
  - **Consumed by**: 2.1.1 strategy dispatch for `peter-attia-drive`
  - [ ] Fetches `https://podcasts.happyscribe.com/the-peter-attia-drive/{slug}` and extracts transcript text
  - [ ] Explicitly detects 403 and marks strategy as `failed` in `feed.transcript_strategy` cache so engine falls through to Whisper
  - [ ] If successful, returns full transcript text

- [ ] **2.1.5** Implement `podcast:transcript` tag checker for generic feeds
  - **Produces**: strategy function; checks `episode.transcript_tag_url` before attempting any other strategy
  - **Consumed by**: 2.1.1 as first strategy in default pipeline
  - [ ] If `transcript_tag_url` is present, fetches URL and returns content (supports `text/plain`, `text/vtt`, `text/html`)
  - [ ] Strips VTT/SRT timestamp lines for `text/vtt` and `application/x-subrip` formats
  - [ ] Returns `(text, "published_transcript")` on success

---

#### [2.2] Whisper Client + Model Routing

- [ ] **2.2.1** Create `whisper_client.py` — HTTP client for whisper-server bridge
  - **Produces**: `scripts/whisper_client.py`; returns transcript text string
  - **Consumed by**: transcript_fetcher.py as `"whisper_large"` and `"whisper_small"` strategies
  - [ ] Downloads episode audio from `episode.audio_url` to `/tmp/podcast-summary/ep_{id}.mp3` using `urllib.request`
  - [ ] POSTs multipart form to `http://localhost:18797/inference` with `file`, `response_format=json`, `language=en`; timeout 1800s (30 min)
  - [ ] Returns `response["text"]` on success; raises on non-200 or empty transcript

- [ ] **2.2.2** Implement model switching for per-show Whisper model routing
  - **Produces**: model-switch logic in whisper_client.py using `POST /load`
  - **Consumed by**: whisper_client.py before each transcription
  - [ ] Checks `feed.whisper_model` (per-show override) or applies default routing: `large-v3` for shows in `WHISPER_LARGE_SHOWS` list, `small.en` for all others
  - [ ] POSTs `{"model": "/path/to/model"}` to `http://localhost:18797/load` before transcribing; restores `small.en` default after
  - [ ] Model paths read from `WHISPER_MODEL_DIR` env var (set in `.env`) so paths are not hardcoded

- [ ] **2.2.3** Add audio download cleanup to whisper_client
  - **Produces**: cleanup logic in whisper_client.py
  - **Consumed by**: whisper_client.py after successful transcription
  - [ ] Deletes `/tmp/podcast-summary/ep_{id}.mp3` after successful transcription
  - [ ] `/tmp/podcast-summary/` directory created if missing; does not fail if cleanup fails (just logs warning)
  - [ ] Skips re-download if audio file already exists in `/tmp/` (supports retry without re-downloading)

---

#### [2.3] Digest Email Composer + Sender

- [ ] **2.3.1** Create `digest_emailer.py` — HTML email composer
  - **Produces**: `scripts/digest_emailer.py`; builds HTML email body string
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py `send_digest()`
  - [ ] Produces one summary card per episode: show name, episode title, source quality label, date, adaptive summary body, episode audio URL as "Listen →" link
  - [ ] Source quality label maps clearly: `published_transcript` → "Full Transcript", `whisper_large` → "Whisper (large)", `whisper_small` → "Whisper (standard)", `show_notes` → "Show Notes Only"
  - [ ] Digest footer includes newsletter archive line: "Health Archive: [N] newsletters stored — [show names]" (or omitted if zero)

- [ ] **2.3.2** Integrate with send_email.py for SMTP delivery
  - **Produces**: `send_digest()` function in engine.py calling `send_email.py`
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py after all episodes processed
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new)
  - [ ] Calls `workspace/skills/send-email/scripts/send_email.py` with `--to`, `--subject`, `--html-body` arguments
  - [ ] Subject format: `🎧 Podcast Digest — N new episodes · {Day Mon DD}`
  - [ ] Email only sent if at least one episode was successfully processed; skipped (with log entry) if nothing new

---

#### [2.4] Summarization via OpenAI API

- [ ] **2.4.1** Create `summarizer.py` — OpenAI API batch summarizer
  - **Produces**: `scripts/summarizer.py`; returns summary string for a given episode + transcript
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py main loop per episode
  - [ ] Calls OpenAI Chat Completions API using `OPENAI_API_KEY` from `.env`; uses `gpt-4o-mini` or configurable model via `PODCAST_SUMMARY_MODEL` env var
  - [ ] Prompt instructs adaptive depth based on `feed.summary_style` (deep_science / long_form_interview / commentary / hunting_outdoor / devotional)
  - [ ] Returns plain text summary (not HTML); engine.py wraps in HTML for digest

- [ ] **2.4.2** Implement auto-classification of `summary_style` for new shows
  - **Produces**: `classify_show_style()` function in summarizer.py
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py when `feed.summary_style` is null
  - [ ] On first episode for a show with no style set: sends show name + description to LLM for classification into one of five style categories; saves result to `feeds.json`
  - [ ] Classification prompt includes the five style definitions from the plan
  - [ ] User can override via Telegram (Phase 4); classification stored in feeds.json is the source of truth

---

#### [2.5] Engine Full Pipeline + OpenClaw Integration

- [ ] **2.5.1** Wire full pipeline in engine.py: poll → fetch → transcribe → summarize → store → email
  - **Produces**: complete `engine.py` processing loop
  - **Consumed by**: system crontab at 11PM
  - [ ] Processes episodes sequentially (not parallel) — whisper-server serializes anyway
  - [ ] Each episode: rss_poller → transcript_fetcher → summarizer → write to episodes.json → accumulate for digest
  - [ ] Writes `processing_status.json` on completion with episode count, show names, completion time, any errors

- [ ] **2.5.2** Enable `podcast-summary` skill in openclaw.json
  - **Produces**: updated `agents/sample-agent/config/openclaw.json`
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent skill routing
  - [ ] `skills.entries` includes `podcast-summary` with `enabled: true`
  - [ ] No new schema keys added — only `skills.entries` modified (per CLAUDE.md constraint)
  - [ ] Agent restart picks up new skill correctly

- [ ] **2.5.3** Add 6AM OpenClaw cron entry for morning notification
  - **Produces**: updated `agents/sample-agent/config/cron/jobs.json`
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw cron scheduler → lightweight agent turn at 6AM
  - [ ] Cron entry: `0 6 * * *` — fires daily at 6AM
  - [ ] Agent turn reads `podcast_vault/processing_status.json` via exec
  - [ ] Sends Telegram/iMessage: "Processed N podcasts [+ M newsletters] overnight — [show names]. Enjoy your digest!" (or "Still processing…" if status is `in_progress`)

- [ ] **2.5.4** Add all Phase 1+2 `.env` keys to sample-agent `.env`
  - **Produces**: documented `.env` additions (keys only, no values)
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py, digest_emailer.py, whisper_client.py
  - [ ] Keys added: `WHISPER_BRIDGE_URL`, `WHISPER_MODEL_DIR`, `PODCAST_DIGEST_TO_EMAIL`, `PODCAST_NOTIFICATION_CHAT_ID`, `PODCAST_SUMMARY_MODEL`
  - [ ] `OPERATIONS.md` updated with description of each key
  - [ ] Existing `OPENAI_API_KEY` and `SMTP_FROM_EMAIL`/`GMAIL_APP_PASSWORD` confirmed as reused (no duplication)

---

### Phase 3: Health Store + Newsletter Ingestion

---

#### [3.1] Health Knowledge Store

- [ ] **3.1.1** Create `health_store.py` — health knowledge CRUD
  - **Produces**: `scripts/health_store.py` with `append_entry()`, `load_all()`, `find_by_show()` functions
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py after summarizing health-tagged episodes; newsletter pipeline (3.4)
  - [ ] `append_entry()` adds one entry to `health_knowledge.json` using atomic write via vault.py
  - [ ] Entry schema: `id`, `show`, `episode_title`, `episode_number`, `date`, `source` (podcast/newsletter), `source_quality`, `topics` (list extracted from summary), `summary`, `tagged_by` (auto/user)
  - [ ] `load_all()` returns all entries sorted by date descending

- [ ] **3.1.2** Implement topic extraction from summaries
  - **Produces**: `extract_topics()` function in health_store.py
  - **Consumed by**: 3.1.1 `append_entry()` before storing
  - [ ] Sends summary text to LLM with a prompt to extract 3–8 health/science topic tags (e.g., "ApoB", "cardiovascular", "sleep", "statins")
  - [ ] Returns list of lowercase string tags; stored as `entry.topics`
  - [ ] Gracefully returns `[]` if extraction fails — does not block storing the entry

---

#### [3.2] Health Tagging Logic

- [ ] **3.2.1** Implement health tier evaluation in engine.py
  - **Produces**: `should_health_tag(feed, summary)` function in engine.py
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py after each summarization — gates health_store.append_entry()
  - [ ] Returns `True` for feeds with `health_tier: "always"` regardless of episode content
  - [ ] Returns `False` for feeds with `health_tier: "never"`
  - [ ] For `health_tier: "sometimes"` (Ferriss, Shawn Ryan): sends summary to LLM for health-relevance classification; returns True/False
  - [ ] Default for unclassified shows: `"never"` until user specifies

- [ ] **3.2.2** Set initial health tier values in feeds.json
  - **Produces**: updated `podcast_vault/feeds.json` with `health_tier` pre-populated
  - **Consumed by**: 3.2.1 tier evaluation
  - [ ] `always`: peter-attia-drive, huberman-lab, found-my-fitness, valley-to-peak-nutrition, barbell-shrugged, better-brain-fitness
  - [ ] `sometimes`: tim-ferriss-show, shawn-ryan-show
  - [ ] All remaining feeds: `never`

---

#### [3.3] Gmail IMAP Newsletter Fetcher

- [ ] **3.3.1** Create `gmail_fetcher.py` — IMAP polling for health-newsletters label
  - **Produces**: `scripts/gmail_fetcher.py`; returns list of newsletter dicts `{subject, body_html, body_text, links, date}`
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py nightly run; 3.4 newsletter content pipeline
  - [ ] Connects via `imaplib.IMAP4_SSL("imap.gmail.com", 993)` with App Password from `GMAIL_IMAP_APP_PASSWORD`
  - [ ] Selects label `"health-newsletters"` (quoted for IMAP); fetches UNSEEN with `BODY.PEEK[]` (no auto-mark-read)
  - [ ] Parses multipart MIME: prefers `text/html` body; falls back to `text/plain`; extracts all `href` links via regex

- [ ] **3.3.2** Implement move-to-Trash on successful processing
  - **Produces**: `mark_processed(mail, msg_id)` in gmail_fetcher.py
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py after newsletter successfully summarized and stored
  - [ ] Copies message to `"[Gmail]/Trash"` then sets `+FLAGS (\Deleted)` on original then calls `EXPUNGE`
  - [ ] Only called after confirmed successful `health_store.append_entry()` — never on partial failure
  - [ ] Failed trash-move is logged as warning but does not fail the run

---

#### [3.4] Newsletter Content Fetcher + Health Store Integration

- [ ] **3.4.1** Implement newsletter full-content fetcher for teaser emails
  - **Produces**: `fetch_newsletter_full_content()` in gmail_fetcher.py
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py newsletter pipeline when body contains a member link
  - [ ] Detects `peterattia.com` or `foundmyfitness.com` member links in email body
  - [ ] Fetches full content using `requests` with session cookie from stored credentials; falls back to Playwright/browser tool if JS-rendering required
  - [ ] Returns full article text; falls back to email body text if fetch fails (with logged warning)

- [ ] **3.4.2** Integrate newsletter pipeline into engine.py
  - **Produces**: `process_newsletters()` function in engine.py
  - **Consumed by**: engine.py main run (alongside episode processing)
  - [ ] Calls `gmail_fetcher.fetch_health_newsletters()`, fetches full content for each, calls `summarizer.summarize()`, calls `health_store.append_entry()`, calls `gmail_fetcher.mark_processed()`
  - [ ] Newsletter count and show names added to `processing_status.json` for 6AM notification
  - [ ] Digest footer updated: "Health Archive: N newsletters stored — [names]"

- [ ] **3.4.3** Add Phase 3 `.env` keys to sample-agent `.env`
  - **Produces**: documented `.env` additions
  - **Consumed by**: gmail_fetcher.py, newsletter content fetcher
  - [ ] Keys added: `GMAIL_IMAP_EMAIL`, `GMAIL_IMAP_APP_PASSWORD`, `ATTIA_SITE_EMAIL`, `ATTIA_SITE_PASSWORD`, `FOUNDMYFITNESS_SITE_EMAIL`, `FOUNDMYFITNESS_SITE_PASSWORD`
  - [ ] `OPERATIONS.md` updated with Gmail label setup instructions ("health-newsletters" label + filter rules)

---

### Phase 4: On-Demand Requests + Telegram Commands

---

#### [4.1] On-Demand Episode Summary (Specific)

- [ ] **4.1.1** Create `on_demand.py` — specific episode lookup and summarization
  - **Produces**: `scripts/on_demand.py`; invoked by agent via exec; emails result
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent turn (SKILL.md pipeline step)
  - [ ] Accepts `--show <name>` + `--episode <number or title>` OR `--url <podcast-url>`; resolves to episode audio URL + metadata
  - [ ] Checks `episodes.json` first (may already be summarized); if found, re-emails existing summary
  - [ ] If not in cache: fetches RSS, finds matching episode, runs full transcript → summarize → email pipeline

- [ ] **4.1.2** Update SKILL.md with on-demand pipeline steps
  - **Produces**: updated `SKILL.md` with numbered on-demand pipeline
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent when user requests an episode summary
  - [ ] Pipeline: classify intent (specific episode / vague topic / re-summarize) → call appropriate on_demand.py mode → confirm email sent
  - [ ] Hard rule: agent never returns summary inline in chat — always emails
  - [ ] Handles "give me a longer summary of that episode" by re-running with `--depth extended`

---

#### [4.2] Semantic Episode Search

- [ ] **4.2.1** Implement vague/topic-based episode search in on_demand.py
  - **Produces**: `--topic <keywords>` mode in on_demand.py
  - **Consumed by**: SKILL.md pipeline when user request is vague ("recent Huberman on vagus nerve")
  - [ ] Fetches last 10 episodes from target show's RSS feed; sends episode titles + descriptions to LLM to find best topic match
  - [ ] If no match in recent 10: extends search to last 30 episodes
  - [ ] Returns matched episode to main pipeline for transcript + summarization

---

#### [4.3] Extended Depth Re-summarization

- [ ] **4.3.1** Implement `--depth extended` mode in on_demand.py + summarizer.py
  - **Produces**: extended depth prompt variant in summarizer.py
  - **Consumed by**: on_demand.py `--depth extended` flag; SKILL.md re-summarize intent
  - [ ] Extended prompt instructs 4–6 paragraph summary with key claims, specific studies/citations mentioned, actionable protocols, and notable quotes
  - [ ] Works on both cached transcripts (from episodes.json) and freshly fetched ones
  - [ ] Stores extended summary separately in episodes.json under `summary_extended` (doesn't overwrite standard summary)

---

#### [4.4] Telegram Feed Management Commands

- [ ] **4.4.1** Document and test "add podcast" Telegram command flow
  - **Produces**: SKILL.md addition + AGENTS.md intent patterns for feed management
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent parsing Telegram/iMessage messages
  - [ ] "Add podcast [name or RSS URL]" → agent finds RSS URL (web_fetch if only name given) → calls `scripts/add_feed.py` via exec → confirms to user
  - [ ] "Stop monitoring [show name]" → sets `state: "inactive"` in feeds.json
  - [ ] "Just get one episode from [show]" → sets `state: "one-off"` for that show

- [ ] **4.4.2** Create `add_feed.py` — single feed add/update utility
  - **Produces**: `scripts/add_feed.py`
  - **Consumed by**: on-demand Telegram commands via exec
  - [ ] Accepts `--name`, `--rss-url`, `--state`, `--health-tier` arguments
  - [ ] Adds new entry to feeds.json if not present; updates existing entry if already there
  - [ ] Prints confirmation JSON for agent to parse and relay to user

---

#### [4.5] Show Classification Feedback Loop

- [ ] **4.5.1** Document and implement Telegram correction flow for summary style
  - **Produces**: SKILL.md addition for style correction intent + `update_feed.py` script
  - **Consumed by**: OpenClaw agent when user gives style feedback
  - [ ] "That Triggernometry summary was too short, give me more detail" → agent sets `feeds.json summary_style` to next deeper tier for that show; re-summarizes if user wants
  - [ ] "Add that Hunt Backcountry episode to the health store" → agent calls `health_store.append_entry()` for the episode with `tagged_by: "user"`
  - [ ] Corrections persist to feeds.json and apply to all future episodes for that show

---

## Execution Strategies

### Sequential Execution
1. **1.1** — Whisper Bridge (no dependencies; must be done before any Whisper transcription)
2. **1.3** — Vault Structure (foundation for all other components)
3. **1.2** — OPML Importer (depends on vault structure)
4. **1.4** — RSS Poller (can start after vault; no other dependencies)
5. **1.5** — Skill Scaffold (can start any time; no code dependencies)
6. **1.6** — Engine Coordinator dry-run (depends on 1.2, 1.3, 1.4)
7. **1.7** — Crontab setup (depends on 1.6)
8. **2.1** — Transcript Fetcher scraping (depends on 1.4)
9. **2.2** — Whisper Client (depends on 1.1)
10. **2.4** — Summarizer (depends on nothing in codebase; can be parallelized with 2.1, 2.2)
11. **2.3** — Digest Emailer (depends on 2.4 for summary format)
12. **2.5** — Engine full pipeline + OpenClaw integration (depends on 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4)
13. **3.1** — Health Store (depends on vault)
14. **3.2** — Health Tagging (depends on 3.1)
15. **3.3** — Gmail Fetcher (depends on vault)
16. **3.4** — Newsletter pipeline integration (depends on 3.1, 3.3)
17. **4.1** — On-demand specific (depends on 2.1, 2.2, 2.4)
18. **4.2** — Semantic search (depends on 4.1)
19. **4.3** — Extended depth (depends on 4.1)
20. **4.4** — Telegram feed management (depends on 1.2)
21. **4.5** — Classification feedback (depends on 2.4, 3.1)

### Parallel Execution

**Wave 1 (concurrent — no dependencies)**
- 1.1 Whisper Bridge, 1.3 Vault Structure, 1.5 Skill Scaffold
- *Rationale: All foundational; none depend on each other*

**Wave 2 (after Wave 1)**
- 1.2 OPML Importer, 1.4 RSS Poller
- *Rationale: Both depend on vault structure (1.3); independent of each other*

**Wave 3 (after Wave 2)**
- 1.6 Engine dry-run, 2.4 Summarizer
- *Rationale: Engine needs RSS poller + vault; summarizer has no codebase dependencies*

**Wave 4 (after Wave 3)**
- 2.1 Transcript Fetcher, 2.2 Whisper Client, 2.3 Digest Emailer
- *Rationale: All depend on engine skeleton (1.6) or summarizer (2.4); independent of each other*

**Wave 5 (after Wave 4)**
- 2.5 Engine full pipeline + OpenClaw integration, 1.7 Crontab setup
- *Rationale: Full pipeline needs all of 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4 complete*

**Wave 6 (after Wave 5 — Phase 3)**
- 3.1 Health Store, 3.3 Gmail Fetcher
- *Rationale: Both need vault; independent of each other*

**Wave 7 (after Wave 6)**
- 3.2 Health Tagging, 3.4 Newsletter Integration
- *Rationale: Both depend on health store (3.1)*

**Wave 8 (after Wave 7 — Phase 4)**
- 4.1 On-demand, 4.4 Feed Management, 4.5 Classification Feedback
- *Rationale: On-demand needs transcript + summarizer; feed management and feedback are independent*

**Wave 9 (after Wave 8)**
- 4.2 Semantic Search, 4.3 Extended Depth
- *Rationale: Both depend on on-demand foundation (4.1)*

---

- Total Requirements Extracted: 26
- Requirements with Task Coverage: 26 (100%)
- Phases: 4
- Parent Tasks: 21
- Sub-tasks: 42
